titleStmt.title: SII 20.53: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 53. (B.K. No. 4 of 1935-36.) LAKSHMĒŚVAR, SHIRHATTI TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab built into the wall of the backyard of the Taluk office.

summary.p: The record is dated the cyclic year Durmati, Pushya, ba. Bhṛigu-shashṭhi, Uttarāyaṇa-Saṁkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1081, December 24.

The inscription, which is incomplete, registers a grant made by Dōṇa, the younger brother of Mahāsāmantādhipati Eṟemayya who was administering the Purigeṟe-300, for the Jaina cult in Purikara. The gift was entrusted to Narēndrasēna (II), of the Sēna-gaṇa in the Mūla-saṅgha, the disciple of Nayasēna Sūri who in his turn was the disciple of Narēndrasēna (I). It is stated that at the time of the record the king's younger brother Yuvarāja Jayasiṁha (III) was governing the two Six-Hundreds (viz. Beḷvola-300 and Purigeṟe-300), Banavāsi-12,000, Sāntaḷige-1000 and Kandūr-1,000.
Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. XVI, pp. 58 ff.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 20.53 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
